raw_sources.title: Adriel Frederick on marginal users, human-in-the-loop algorithms, and growth discipline
raw_sources.guest: Adriel Frederick
raw_sources.relevance_to_flywheel: Marginal user methodology + human judgment in algorithmic systems + growth experiment portfolio discipline

raw_sources.title: Aishwarya Naresh Reganti + Kiriti Badam on AI product design, non-determinism, and CCCD
raw_sources.relevance_to_flywheel: AI product non-determinism + agency/control trade-offs + continuous calibration continuous development (CCCD) framework + evals vs production monitoring + behavioral flywheel loops

raw_sources.title: Amol Avasare — Anthropic growth, CASH, and the squeezed PM
raw_sources.source: Lenny's Podcast (Dropbox archive shared by Gaurav)
raw_sources.guest: Amol Avasare (Head of Growth, Anthropic; ex-Mercury, MasterClass)
raw_sources.relevance_to_flywheel: HIGH. Different person from Amole Naik — same Anthropic growth org, deeper on operating model. Confirms CASH (Claude Accelerates Sustainable Hypergrowth) is the canonical reference for substrate-led growth experimentation. Adds the "squeezed PM" thesis (engineering 2-3x'd by Claude Code, PM/design now bottleneck) and the four-stage automation loop (identify → build → test → analyze; cross-functional alignment is the holdout).

raw_sources.title: Anthropic is automating its own growth
raw_sources.guest: Amole Naik (Head of Growth, Anthropic; ex-Mercury, Masterclass)
raw_sources.relevance_to_flywheel: This is the closest episode to the flywheel pitch. CASH (Claude Accelerates Sustainable Hypergrowth) is the literal blueprint — automate growth experimentation across 4 stages with human-in-loop on stakeholder alignment + brand. Almost everything Kesava is building maps to a piece of Amole's stack.

raw_sources.title: Andrew Wilkinson - Niche Markets, Lazy Leadership, and AI Automation
raw_sources.guest: Andrew Wilkinson
raw_sources.relevance_to_flywheel: Founder-level startup idea formation, competitive positioning via niches vs. crowded markets, lazy leadership (delegation), AI automation of routine knowledge work.

raw_sources.title: Andy Raskin — Strategic Narrative Framework (Old Game to New Game)
raw_sources.guest: Andy Raskin
raw_sources.relevance_to_flywheel: Positioning framework, narrative structure, go-to-market messaging, CEO alignment, sales-marketing-product coherence.

raw_sources.title: Annie Duke — Decision Quality, Explicit Thinking, Feedback Loops
raw_sources.guest: Annie Duke
raw_sources.relevance_to_flywheel: Decision quality, pre-mortems, shortening feedback loops, mental time travel, leadership communication (nevertheless).

raw_sources.title: Anton Osika on Lovable — the last piece of software
raw_sources.guest: Anton Osika
raw_sources.relevance_to_flywheel: AI-native product teams obsess over UX/taste over engineering; scaling via generalists + shared office lunches; prioritize bottleneck identification, not roadmap theater

raw_sources.title: anuj rathi — full-stack pm, working backwards, india marketplace dynamics
Aparna Chennapragada — NLX is the new UX, living in the future, taste over roadmaps
raw_sources.guest: Aparna Chennapragada
raw_sources.relevance_to_flywheel: Natural language interface becomes UX substrate; taste + territorial thinking replaces roadmap theater; "living one year in the future" operationalized via Frontier program

raw_sources.title: april dunford - positioning authority, 200+ companies
raw_sources.guest: April Dunford

raw_sources.title: april dunford — positioning, setup follow-through, differentiation
archie abrams - churn optimization & long-term holdout experiments

raw_sources.title: Asha Sharma — Product as organism, post-training loops, agentic society
raw_sources.guest: Asha Sharma
raw_sources.relevance_to_flywheel: Post-training is new moat; product metabolism = data ingest + reward model + outcome tuning; org chart becomes work chart; all software will be model-forward

raw_sources.title: ayo omojola — differentiation, design depth, going deep, regulated industries
Bangaly Kaba — Understand work, adjacent user theory, impact frame (environment + skills)
raw_sources.guest: Bangaly Kaba
raw_sources.relevance_to_flywheel: Understand before identify-execute; environment + skills = impact potential; adjacent user is the growth lever; taste is learned

raw_sources.title: Dr. Becky Kennedy — Good Inside leadership, repair, MGI, behavior vs identity
raw_sources.guest: Dr. Becky Kennedy (clinical psychologist; founder, Good Inside)
raw_sources.relevance_to_flywheel: Operating-norm playbook for high-trust teams under pressure — separate behavior from identity, repair as primary relationship strategy, most generous interpretation, sturdy leadership. Direct read for managing the JustCall PMM team during the substrate transition and the exit window.

raw_sources.title: Marketplace liquidity, supply control, and mentorship scaling
raw_sources.guest: Benjamin Lauzier (VP Product, Lyft + Thumbtack)
raw_sources.relevance_to_flywheel: marketplace-supply-flywheel, growth-engineering-patterns

raw_sources.title: Benjamin Mann — Anthropic, the Economic Turing Test, mission over money, transformative AI
raw_sources.guest: Benjamin Mann (co-founder Anthropic; tech lead product engineering; ex-OpenAI GPT-3 architect)
raw_sources.relevance_to_flywheel: Mann's Economic Turing Test gives a precise external benchmark for "AI-native" claims. His "use the new tools as if they were new tools" rule is the operating discipline Kesava is enforcing on the team. The mission-over-money frame is a calibration for Anthropic-class talent retention, applicable to clearPMM and to the why-Kesava-stays-on-flywheel framing.

raw_sources.title: What happens after coding is solved
raw_sources.guest: Boris Cherny (Head of Claude Code, Anthropic)
raw_sources.relevance_to_flywheel: Direct parallel to flywheel — engineering's "everyone codes" → marketing's "everyone is a builder"; latent demand + bet-on-six-months-out + underfund mechanisms.

raw_sources.title: Agent futures, outcomes-based pricing, Google Local to Maps
raw_sources.guest: Bret Taylor (ex-CTO Meta, ex-co-CEO Salesforce, Chair OpenAI)
raw_sources.relevance_to_flywheel: agent-market-structure, pricing-model-shift

raw_sources.title: Brian Balfour on distribution platform cycles and the ChatGPT opportunity
raw_sources.guest: Brian Balfour
raw_sources.relevance_to_flywheel: Distribution strategy, platform cycles, escape velocity, startup timing

raw_sources.title: Brian Halligan — CEO craft, the LOCK(S) algorithm, distribution as the new moat
raw_sources.guest: Brian Halligan (co-founder and longtime CEO, HubSpot; in-house CEO coach at Sequoia)
raw_sources.relevance_to_flywheel: Operator-grade hiring rubric (LOCK + Student); homegrown vs Big-Tech-import bias; distribution as the new moat thesis (echoes Spiegel/Fain); peer-room learning model that maps onto commons rooms.

raw_sources.title: How Anthropic's product team moves faster than anyone else
raw_sources.guest: Cat Wu (Head of Product, Claude Code + Co-work, Anthropic)

raw_sources.title: Claire Hughes Johnson — scaling people, the company operating system, force for positive momentum
raw_sources.guest: Claire Hughes Johnson (ex-COO Stripe, ex-VP Google self-driving / global online sales; author of "Scaling People")
raw_sources.relevance_to_flywheel: Direct external precedent for the operating-cadence layer of the flywheel substrate. CHJ's house metaphor (foundation / supporting beams / mechanicals), the operating-principles framework, and the "force for positive momentum" decision rule are all literal templates for the AI-native marketing team operating model the flywheel pitches.

raw_sources.title: Claire Vo — OpenClaw, agents as a team, manager skill as the unlock
raw_sources.guest: Claire Vo (host of How I AI; 3x CPO; founder, ChatPRD)
raw_sources.relevance_to_flywheel: Multi-agent specialization (one agent per role, not one for everything); manager skillset is the unlock; tunable plain-language onboarding; treat agents like employees with progressive trust.

raw_sources.title: Elena Verna 3.0 — 10 growth tactics that never work, earned channels, optionality
raw_sources.guest: Elena Verna (head of growth at Dropbox / Miro / Amplitude / SurveyMonkey; advisor to ~50+ B2B SaaS)
raw_sources.relevance_to_flywheel: Direct PMM/growth read for JustCall flywheel and clearPMM offer. Elena's earned-channel thesis maps to the substrate-as-leverage frame; her experimentation-paralysis warning maps to "every initiative an experiment" failure mode in Q1 reorg; her optionality vs. titles thesis aligns with Kesava's exit ramp and 4-tier clearPMM structure.

raw_sources.title: Evan Spiegel — Distribution is the moat, design is the bottleneck
raw_sources.guest: Evan Spiegel (CEO and co-founder, Snap)
raw_sources.relevance_to_flywheel: Software-isn't-a-moat thesis; design as bottleneck for cohesion; jobs-to-be-done as the spine for agentizing whole workflows; flat innovation team coupled to large operational org.

raw_sources.title: Hamel Husain & Shreya Shankar — evals as error analysis, the benevolent dictator, LLM judges
raw_sources.guest: Hamel Husain (consultant, ex-Github/Airbnb ML) + Shreya Shankar (Berkeley PhD; "Who Validates the Validated?" research)
raw_sources.relevance_to_flywheel: Evals are the load-bearing missing skill for the AI Growth lane. Hamel + Shreya's open-coding → axial-coding → LLM-as-judge pipeline is the literal substrate for measuring flywheel output quality, for the JustCall AI agents (Fin parallel), and for the contextDB review loop. Direct external benchmark for the marketing-substrate eval shape.

raw_sources.title: Jacob Warwick — comp negotiation, information asymmetry, sell the vacation
raw_sources.guest: Jacob Warwick (fingerprintless executive negotiator, $1B+ in client comp)
raw_sources.relevance_to_flywheel: Direct read for Kesava's 12-week exit ramp comp tactics; also a positioning playbook (consultative discovery, sell the vacation, plant champions) that maps cleanly to clearPMM enterprise sales motion and to flywheel pitch structure.

raw_sources.title: Jeetu Patel — Turning Cisco AI-first, stamina over intellect, criticize in public
raw_sources.guest: Jeetu Patel (Chief Product Officer and President, Cisco; ex-Box, ex-Documentum)
raw_sources.relevance_to_flywheel: Large-org AI transformation playbook (90K employees); permission-to-play strategy filter; treat AI as a teammate not a tool; criticize-in-public norm for high-trust teams.

raw_sources.title: The design process is dead. Here's what's replacing it.
raw_sources.guest: Jenny Wen (Head of Design, Claude Co-work; ex-Figma Director, Dropbox, Square, Shopify)
raw_sources.relevance_to_flywheel: Design's mock-and-prototype phase shrank from 60-70% to 30-40% of the role; the rest is jamming with engineers, polishing in code, and pointing to vision. Direct mirror of where PMM is heading.

raw_sources.title: Jessica Fain — How to influence executives (and why it's not politics)
raw_sources.guest: Jessica Fain (VP Product at Webflow; ex-Brightwheel, ex-Slack chief of staff, ex-Box)
raw_sources.relevance_to_flywheel: Tactical playbook for upward influence — stakeholder discovery, incentive alignment, kill-things-to-build-trust, shrink-the-change, agents-as-junior-colleagues. Direct read for flywheel pitches to Deepan/CFO and for substrate teammate onboarding.

raw_sources.title: Growth tactics from OpenAI and Stripe's first marketer
raw_sources.guest: Krithika Shankarraman (Executive in Residence, Thrive Capital; ex-VP Marketing OpenAI; first marketer at Stripe, Retool; ex-Google, Dropbox)
raw_sources.relevance_to_flywheel: PMM operator voice from someone who was solo-marketer at Stripe for 3 years and first marketer at OpenAI. The DATE diagnostic framework is a direct competitor/replacement for playbook-thinking — and her chamoeleon-CMO + comb-shape framing maps to flywheel's hiring needs.

raw_sources.title: Mihika Kapoor — vision, conviction, building hype, scope is the world
raw_sources.guest: Mihika Kapoor (design-engineering-PM hybrid at Figma; FigJam early PM; spearheading new product launch)
raw_sources.relevance_to_flywheel: Mihika's playbook for shipping zero-to-one inside a big company maps directly onto the flywheel build inside JustCall. Scope-is-the-world ownership, vision-as-pain+solution+proof, A-minus-idea conviction, and internal hype mechanics are the operating tactics Kesava is already using and needs to formalize for the team.

raw_sources.title: Qasar Younis — Physical AI, radical pragmatism, build quietly
raw_sources.guest: Qasar Younis (CEO and co-founder, Applied Intuition; ex-COO at YC)
raw_sources.relevance_to_flywheel: Build-quietly counter-narrative to "build in public"; nuance over polarization; AI-native mindset for industries with chronic labor shortages; framing as "intelligence put into things that already exist."

raw_sources.title: Sherwin Wu — Codex inside OpenAI, engineers as managers, build for where the model is going
raw_sources.guest: Sherwin Wu (Head of Engineering, OpenAI API and Developer Platform)
raw_sources.relevance_to_flywheel: Direct engineering analog of the flywheel thesis — ICs become "tech leads of agent fleets"; context engineering > prompt tweaking; build for the next model not today's; one-person-billion-dollar startup creates B2B SaaS golden age.

raw_sources.title: Simon Willison — Agentic engineering and the November inflection
raw_sources.source: Lenny's Podcast (Dropbox archive shared by Gaurav)
raw_sources.guest: Simon Willison (co-creator of Django, creator of Datasette, prolific blogger)
raw_sources.relevance_to_flywheel: HIGH. Simon names the November 2025 inflection point (GPT 5.1 + Opus 4.5 crossed "almost always works" threshold) and provides the cleanest articulation of the agentic engineering pattern stack — dark factory testing, hoarding patterns, Red/Green TDD, template starts. Also: explicit prediction that the bottleneck has migrated from coding to everywhere else, and the "Challenger disaster" warning on responsible deployment.
